'''''Star Wars'' 96''' is the ninety-sixth issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Publisher's summary==
''There's a new Dark Lord in the galaxy, and she's out for Luke's head! Not only must Luke fight Lumiya, he must also deal with who and, more dangerously, ''what'' she really is! "Duel With a Dark Lady!" written by Jo Duffy, penciled by Cynthia Martin and inked by Bob Wiacek''.<ref name="ma" />

==Plot summary==
[[File:Duel_on_Kinooine.jpg|thumb|left|Luke Skywalker battling Lumiya.]]

[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] [[Skirmish on Kinooine|faces off]] with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lady]] [[Lumiya]] on [[Kinooine/Legends|Kinooine]]. Lumiya and her [[Lightwhip/Legends|lightwhip]] soundly defeat Luke, whom Lumiya then captures; she has already captured the [[Zeltron/Legends|Zeltron]], [[Dani/Legends|Dani]]. Meanwhile, [[Kiro]], a water-breathing [[Iskalonian]] who had already been defeated by Lumiya and left for dead, jury-rigs breathing equipment and goes to rescue Luke and Dani. He finds Luke first, and Luke formulates a plan to defeat Lumiya. The two find her, and Luke reclaims his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] from her with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Lumiya expects to defeat him again, only Luke reveals his plan - he has created a [[Shoto lightsaber/Legends|shoto]] which, in concert with his lightsaber, allow him to match Lumiya's lightwhip. Luke manages to rip the whip from her hands, and she charges him, enraged; he sidesteps and cuts half of her armor off, revealing the face beneath, that of [[Lumiya|Shira Brie]]. Luke tries to reach out to the good in her, but before he has any success, the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] invasion fleet appears out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in the skies overhead . . .

==Reception==
{{Quote|Artist Cynthia Martin's design of a "female Vader" is so striking that the character enthralls readers at first glance. With no sound and no words, Luke's Jedi lightsaber proves deficient against the bizarre and spellbinding power of Lumiya's malleable Sith lightwhip.|Abel G. Peña and Enrique Guerrero|<ref name="20 Most Memorable Moments">{{InsiderCite|83|20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe}}</ref>}}
In [[2005]], the magazine article "[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]," written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Enrique Guerrero]] and published in [[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]], named ''Star Wars'' 96's opening duel between Luke Skywalker and Lumiya as one of its titular most memorable moments. They praised writer [[Mary Jo Duffy]]'s decision to write a wordless duel that "lets the panels of the comic do the talking" and asserted that "artist [[Cynthia Martin]]'s design of a '[[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Vader]]' is so striking that the character enthralls readers at first glance."<ref name="20 Most Memorable Moments" />

Tegan O'Neil of [[Wikipedia:The A.V. Club|The A.V. Club]] similarly praised Martin's work in a [[2015]] retrospective of [[Marvel Comics|Marvel's]] [[Star Wars (1977)|original ''Star Wars'' series]], captioning art of Lumiya from ''Star Wars'' 96 with the claim that "Cynthia Martin's manga influenced art was a highlight of the series' last year."<ref name="AV Club 2015">{{WebCite|url=https://www.avclub.com/a-really-long-time-ago-marvel-played-fast-and-loose-wi-1798286716|text=A really long time ago, Marvel played fast and loose with Star Wars|work=[[Wikipedia:The A.V. Club|The A.V. Club]]|date=2015-11-21|author=Tegan O'Neil|archivedate=20220712223204}}</ref> [[Jason Fry]], too, commented on the anime-inspired look of the Nagai that debuted in ''Star Wars'' 96—he profiled the issue in his [[Star Wars Insider 91|''Star Wars Insider'' 91]] article "[[Comic Marvels]]" ([[2006]]), while also noting that the invading Nagai fleet seen in the closing pages predicted a trope that would recur [[Ssi-ruu|numerous]] [[Yuuzhan Vong|times]] in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].<ref name="Comic Marvels">{{InsiderCite|91|Comic Marvels}}
</ref>

==Legacy==
{{Quote|The shoto was a special half-length lightsaber that Luke had built after nearly losing his life the first time he encountered Lumiya's lightwhip. The shorter blade allowed him to fight in the Jar'Kai style—with a weapon in each hand—which counteracted the advantage of the lightwhip's dual-natured strands of energy and matter.|Narration in the 2006 novel ''Legacy of the Force: Tempest''|<ref name="Tempest">''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]''</ref>}}
Lumiya appeared several more times in Marvel Comics' ongoing ''Star Wars'' series,<ref name="Star Wars 97">[[Star Wars (1977) 97|''Star Wars'' (1977) 97]]</ref><ref name="Star Wars 100">[[Star Wars (1977) 100|''Star Wars'' (1977) 100]]</ref> and she was ultimately defeated by Luke Skywalker in its [[Star Wars (1977) 107|final issue]].<ref name="Star Wars 107">[[Star Wars (1977) 107|''Star Wars'' (1977) 107]]</ref> In [[2006]], she returned to the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]] in the [[List of books|novel series]] [[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force|''Legacy of the Force'']],<ref name="Betrayal">''[[Legacy of the Force: Betrayal]]''</ref> and her duel with Skywalker on Kinooine was referenced in the series' third book, [[Legacy of the Force: Tempest|''Tempest'']].<ref name="Tempest" />

The shoto lightsaber that was invented by author [[Mary Jo Duffy]] for ''Star Wars'' 96 went on to have a robust lifespan in ''[[Star Wars]]'' media over the years, being re-wielded by Skywalker in the aforementioned ''Legacy of the Force'' novels<ref name="Tempest" /> and later emerging as a choice weapon of the [[Jedi]] [[Ahsoka Tano]] in the animated ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' TV series,<ref name="Heroes on Both Sides">{{TCW|Heroes on Both Sides}}</ref> and again in live-action TV shows like [[Star Wars: The Mandalorian|''The Mandalorian'']]<ref name="The Jedi">{{TheMandalorian|Chapter 13: The Jedi}}</ref> and the eponymous [[Star Wars: Ahsoka|''Ahsoka'']].<ref name="Master and Apprentice">{{Ahsoka|Part One: Master and Apprentice}}</ref> The [[Yoda's lightsaber|lightsaber]] used by the diminutive [[Yoda]] in the ''Star Wars'' [[Prequel trilogy]] was also retconned to be a shoto.<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars">''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]''</ref>
